document.title: Final Inspection Test Report
standards: Medical Device Standards Regulation 15. Medical X-ray Equipment 9.7(1)
Medical Device Standards Regulation 15. Medical X-ray Equipment 9.8 (2)
Medical Device Standards Regulation 15. Medical X-ray Equipment 10.2(5)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Final inspection test report for Portable X-ray model HnX-P1, S/N HnXP1202512160002.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents final inspection, safety testing, X-ray performance testing, and measuring equipment used for the Portable X-ray device.
keyPoints: Product Name is Portable X-ray and Model is HnX-P1.
Portable X-ray S/N is HnXP1202512160002 and Date of Manufacture is 25-12-16.
Inspection date is 25-12-16 and inspector is PooLeum, Kim.

Appearance, motion, weight, safety, tube voltage, tube current, irradiation time, timer, dose reproducibility and linearity, leakage dose, half-level, collimator, radiation control, and light beam difference tests are reported as PASS where stated.

evidence.evidenceText: Final Inspection Test Report

evidence.evidenceText: inspection date 25-12-16 inspector PooLeum, Kim

evidence.claim: The report lists Inverter S/N IV2506090107, Battery S/N CI250055, X-ray Tube S/N 732883, and HV Tank Ass'y S/N HV2506090107.
evidence.evidenceText: Inverter IV2506090107 - Battery CI250055 - X-ray Tube 732883 - HV Tank Ass'y HV2506090107 -

evidence.claim: The F/W Version is 00.12 ver.
evidence.evidenceText: F/W Version 00.12 ver -

evidence.claim: Appearance inspection criteria include particle defect, scratch, dent, poor printing, and dimensional limits for front, back, and aspect surfaces.
evidence.evidenceText: Particle Defect, Scratch, Dent, Poor printing Front (0.6mm↓), Back (0.8mm↓), Aspect(1.0mm↓) PASS

evidence.claim: Product label printing status and bar code recognition passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Product label printing status and bar code recognition There will be no break in product label printing, erasure, or abnormal bar code recognition. PASS

evidence.claim: Motion inspection for power on/off, LCD, operation button, and display status passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Motion inspection Power on/off, LCD operation button Display status There should be no abnormalities in power on/off and operation status (function button operation). PASS

evidence.claim: Portable X-ray weight without battery was 3.1 kg and passed the 3.1kg ±0.2kg specification.
evidence.evidenceText: Portable X-ray weight SPEC 3.1kg ±0.2kg ( Without Battery ) 3.1 kg PASS

evidence.claim: Withstand voltage testing passed at AC 3000 V for 60 seconds with 0.9 mA against a ≤10 mA specification.
evidence.evidenceText: Withstand voltage test AC 3000 V 60sec ≤ 10mA 0.9 mA PASS

evidence.claim: A touch current test under IEC 60601-1 Clause 8.7.4 was marked not applicable because the enclosure is flame-retardant polycarbonate and has no accessible conductive exterior parts.
evidence.evidenceText: The enclosure of the equipment is made of flame-retardant polycarbonate (PC), which is an insulating material. Since there are no accessible conductive parts on the exterior surface, the touch current test according to IEC 60601-1, Clause 8.7.4, is not applicable (N/A).

evidence.claim: Power input testing passed with 47 W, 99 VA, and 242 V reported.
evidence.evidenceText: Power Input Test Rated Voltage 110%, 10sec Power consumption value (242V, 40sec) 47 W PASS 99 VA PASS 242 V PASS

evidence.claim: Ground leakage current testing passed with 0.04 mA for both normal condition and single fault condition.
evidence.evidenceText: Ground leak current test Rated Voltage ±10% N/C ≤ 5mA 0.04 mA PASS S/F ≤ 10mA 0.04 mA PASS

evidence.claim: Tube voltage testing used IEC60601-2-54 accuracy criteria for X-ray tube voltage.
evidence.evidenceText: Test criteria : IEC60601-2-54(Accuracy of X-RAY Tube VOLATAGE)

evidence.claim: Tube voltage test results passed for 50.0kV, 60.0kV, 70.0kV, 80.0kV, and 90.0kV at 10mAs.
Tube current testing used IEC60601-2-54 accuracy criteria for X-ray tube current.
evidence.evidenceText: Test criteria : IEC60601-2-54(Accuracy of X-RAY Tube CURRENT)

evidence.claim: Tube current test results passed for 50kV/10mAs, 70kV/8mAs, and 90kV/10mAs conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: 50kV / 10mAs ±10% or less 0.990V PASS 70kV / 8mAs 0.993V PASS 90kV / 10mAs 0.798V PASS

evidence.claim: Irradiation time testing used IEC60601-2-54 accuracy criteria for irradiation time.
evidence.evidenceText: Test criteria : IEC60601-2-54(Accuracy of IRRADIATION TIME)

evidence.claim: Irradiation time test results passed for 60kV/1mAs, 60kV/20mAs, 90kV/0.8mAs, and 90kV/16mAs conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: 60kV / 1mAs ±10% or less 0.106 6.00 PASS 60kV / 20mAs 2.053 2.65 PASS 90kV / 0.8mAs 0.109 9.00 PASS 90kV / 16mAs 2.062 3.10 PASS

evidence.claim: Current time product testing used IEC60601-2-54 accuracy criteria.
evidence.evidenceText: Test criteria : IEC60601-2-54(Accuracy of of Current Time Product)

evidence.claim: Dose reproducibility and linearity testing used IEC60601-2-54 reproducibility and linearity criteria.
evidence.evidenceText: Test criteria : IEC60601-2-54(Reproducibility and Linearity)
evidence.pageTarget: X-ray Dose Reproducibility and Linearity Testing

evidence.claim: Dose reproducibility assessment criterion was CV≤0.05 and the reported CV values passed.
evidence.evidenceText: CV coefficient of variation 0.011 0.008 0.010 0.007 0.007 0.008 Reproducibility assessment CV≤0.05 PASS PASS
evidence.pageTarget: X-ray Dose Reproducibility and Linearity Testing

evidence.claim: Linearity judgment was OK.
evidence.evidenceText: Linearity judgment OK
evidence.pageTarget: X-ray Dose Reproducibility and Linearity Testing

evidence.claim: Leakage dose testing references Medical Device Standards 15 - Medical X-ray equipment 9.7(1).
evidence.evidenceText: Medical Device Standards 15 - Medical X-ray equipment 9.7(1)
evidence.pageTarget: Medical Device Standards Regulation 15 Medical X-ray Equipment

evidence.claim: Leakage dose testing used load condition 90kV, 8mA, 2Sec => 16mAs.
evidence.evidenceText: Load condition 90kV, 8mA, 2Sec => 16mAs

evidence.claim: Half-level test under 90kV, 16mAs, 50cm position reported 3.7 mm against a 3.2 mm or greater specification and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Test conditions: 90kV, 16mAs, 50cm position Measure the values before and after adding AL 3.6mm. 3.2 mm ↑ 3.7 mm PASS

evidence.claim: Collimator performance testing passed max X-ray light field more than 35×35 cm at SID 65 cm.
evidence.evidenceText: Max. X-ray light field more than 35×35㎝ at SID 65㎝ PASS

evidence.claim: Collimator performance testing passed min X-ray light field less than 5×5 cm at SID 100 cm.
evidence.evidenceText: Min. X-ray light field less than 5×5㎝ at SID100㎝ PASS

evidence.claim: Collimator illuminance criterion more than 160 Lux at SID 100 cm passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Illuminance more than 160Lux at SID 100㎝ PASS

evidence.claim: X-ray radiation control test requires stopping X-ray examination if the hand switch is opened during examination, and visual confirmation passed.
evidence.evidenceText: If the hand switch is opened during X-ray examination, the X-ray examination should stop. Visual confirmation PASS

evidence.claim: X-ray light beam difference testing uses e1+e2≤10mm and e3+e4≤10mm judgment criteria and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Measures the difference between the light range of an X-ray and the beam of a collimator. Within the margin of error e1+e2≤10mm e3+e4≤10mm

evidence.claim: The report lists measuring equipment including dosimeter G13-0172, leakage current meter 12AK089 / 169, frequency converter 6683, oscilloscope JY930/0206, illuminometer 150303575, multimeter 25630415, electronic scale H13-19832, multimeter 41710178, and CE multimeter 12221400.
evidence.evidenceText: Measuring equipment name S/N note. Dosimeter G13-0172 Leakage current meter 12AK089 / 169 Frequency converter 6683 OSCILLOSCOPE JY930/0206 illuminometer 150303575 Multimeter 25630415 Electronic scale H13-19832 Multimeter 41710178 CE MULTIMETER 12221400

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly identified; source metadata authoritySignal is general and the document only references standards or regulations.
Document date is shown as 25-12-16 without an explicit century or date format expansion.
The source text appears to be OCR or preprocessed extraction with some ordering and wording artifacts, including 'investigation time' for irradiation time and fragmented table rows.
